title: Lock S3 with Block Public Access, bucket policy, and default encryption
description: Keep account and bucket-level Block Public Access on, deny non-TLS and unencrypted PutObject in the bucket policy, and set SSE-KMS default encryption.
body: Use when creating an S3 bucket or reviewing object exposure. Do not disable Block Public Access to share data and avoid bucket policies with Principal:* unless serving genuinely public static content. Actions: keep BlockPublicAcls/IgnorePublicAcls/BlockPublicPolicy/RestrictPublicBuckets all true, add a bucket policy that denies aws:SecureTransport false and denies PutObject without s3:x-amz-server-side-encryption, set default SSE-KMS with a scoped key, and confirm with s3api get-bucket-policy-status. Validation signals include get-bucket-policy-status IsPublic false, get-bucket-encryption returning the KMS rule, and a denied plaintext-HTTP GET. Risk boundary: any change that makes a bucket or object public is human-gated and must be logged.
